frame_analyses.description: Already formed extracorporeal knot visible outside trainer, white suture material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears pre-formed, ready for deployment

frame_analyses.description: Using knot pusher to advance knot down suture strands into trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper knot pusher technique, maintaining tension on both strands

frame_analyses.description: Continuing to advance knot with pusher, overhead view shows trainer box
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good control of knot pusher, maintaining suture orientation

frame_analyses.description: Knot being positioned at target site, both suture ends visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Careful positioning to ensure proper knot placement

frame_analyses.description: Final positioning of knot, appears to be at appropriate location on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears seated, ready for securing

frame_analyses.description: Using scissors to cut excess suture material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cutting technique, leaving appropriate tail length

frame_analyses.description: Continuing to trim suture ends to appropriate length
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Careful trimming to avoid cutting too close to knot

frame_analyses.description: Final trimming of suture tails, task appears complete
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion with both tails cut to appropriate length

confidence_rationale: Camera angle and lighting make it difficult to assess suture placement accuracy relative to marks on the Penrose drain. Knot formation and cutting phases are clearly visible but precise measurement of deviation from marks cannot be determined.
cannot_determine: Exact deviation from marks on Penrose drain - marks not clearly visible
Precise gap measurement in drain - viewing angle limitations
Exact needle placement phase - video appears to start after needle placement
technique_summary: Competent performance with particular time loss in knot pushing phase (145s vs 30-60s expert benchmark). Clean extracorporeal knot formation and appropriate suture cutting technique.
strengths: Clean suture cutting with appropriate tail length
improvement_suggestions: Reduce time in knot pushing phase through practice
Work on more efficient knot advancement technique
